Apple - Apple Scab.name: Apple Scab
symptoms: Olive-green to brown spots on leaves
Velvety or fuzzy appearance on spots
Scabby, corky brown lesions on fruit
Apple - Apple Scab.treatment: Apply sulfur or copper-based fungicides. For organic control, use Neem oil or Serenade (Bacillus subtilis). Remove and destroy fallen leaves to prevent overwintering.
Apple - Apple Scab.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Prune trees to open the canopy.
Apple - Apple Scab.immediate_action: Remove affected leaves and fruit. Avoid overhead watering to keep foliage dry.

Apple - Black Rot.name: Apple - Black Rot
symptoms: Purple spots on leaves (frogeye leaf spot)
Large, concentric brown circles on fruit
Fruit eventually turns black and shrivels (mummy)
Apple - Black Rot.treatment: Prune out infected branches and cankers. Apply fungicides containing captan or sulfur.
Apple - Black Rot.prevention: Remove 'mummified' fruit from trees and ground. Sanitize pruning tools between cuts.
Apple - Black Rot.immediate_action: Prune out dead wood and remove infected fruit immediately.

Apple - Cedar Apple Rust.name: Apple - Cedar Apple Rust
symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface
Orange-brown 'tubes' on leaf underside later in season
Apple - Cedar Apple Rust.treatment: Apply sulfur or copper fungicides in early spring. Organic options include Neem oil.
Apple - Cedar Apple Rust.prevention: Remove nearby cedar trees if possible. Plant resistant apple varieties.
Apple - Cedar Apple Rust.immediate_action: Spray affected trees with an appropriate fungicide as soon as spots appear.

Apple - Healthy.name: Apple - Healthy
symptoms: No visible spots, lesions, or wilting
Apple - Healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Maintain current care routine.
Apple - Healthy.prevention: Continue regular watering, fertilization, and monitoring.
Apple - Healthy.immediate_action: None required. Enjoy your healthy plant!

Blueberry - Healthy.name: Blueberry - Healthy
Blueberry - Healthy.causes: Healthy growing environment with acidic soil.
Blueberry - Healthy.prevention: Maintain soil pH between 4.5 and 5.2. Mulch to retain moisture.

Cherry - Healthy.name: Cherry - Healthy
Cherry - Healthy.immediate_action: Monitor for common pests like aphids.

Cherry - Powdery Mildew.name: Cherry - Powdery Mildew
symptoms: Fruit may be covered in white fuzz
Cherry - Powdery Mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate sprays. Organic: Use a mix of milk and water (1:9 ratio).
Cherry - Powdery Mildew.prevention: Improve air circulation through pruning. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilizer.
Cherry - Powdery Mildew.immediate_action: Increase sunlight exposure if possible and apply an organic fungicide.

Corn (Maize) - Cercospora Leaf Spot.name: Corn - Cercospora Leaf Spot (Gray Leaf Spot)
symptoms: Small, rectangular tan to gray spots
Spots merge to form large blighted areas
Leaves eventually turn brown and die
Corn (Maize) - Cercospora Leaf Spot.treatment: Apply foliar fungicides like strobilurins or triazoles if infection is severe.
Corn (Maize) - Cercospora Leaf Spot.prevention: Crop rotation with non-host crops. Tillage to bury infected residue. Plant resistant hybrids.
Corn (Maize) - Cercospora Leaf Spot.immediate_action: Scout fields regularly. Consider early harvest if stalks are weakened.

Corn (Maize) - Common Rust.name: Corn - Common Rust
symptoms: Small, cinnamon-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces
Pustules turn black as the plant matures
Corn (Maize) - Common Rust.treatment: Rarely requires treatment in field corn, but fungicides can be used in sweet corn.
Corn (Maize) - Common Rust.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Early planting can help avoid peak spore periods.
Corn (Maize) - Common Rust.immediate_action: Monitor progress. In most cases, the yield impact is minimal.

Corn (Maize) - Healthy.name: Corn - Healthy
Corn (Maize) - Healthy.prevention: Maintain soil health and consistent watering.

Corn (Maize) - Northern Leaf Blight.name: Corn - Northern Leaf Blight
symptoms: Lesions are tan or gray and have pointed ends
Corn (Maize) - Northern Leaf Blight.treatment: Foliar fungicides if economic threshold is reached.
Corn (Maize) - Northern Leaf Blight.prevention: Resistant hybrids, crop rotation, and residue management.
Corn (Maize) - Northern Leaf Blight.immediate_action: Identify the severity to decide on fungicide application.

Grape - Black Rot.name: Grape - Black Rot
symptoms: Small brown spots on leaves with black borders
Berries turn brown and سپس shrivel into black 'mummies'
Small black dots (pycnidia) on spots and mummies
Grape - Black Rot.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Myclobutanil fungicides. Organic: Copper or sulfur sprays.
Grape - Black Rot.prevention: Remove all mummified berries from vines and ground. Prune to improve airflow.
Grape - Black Rot.immediate_action: Remove infected clusters immediately to prevent spread.

Grape - Esca (Black Measles).name: Grape - Esca
symptoms: Sudden wilting of the entire vine (apoplexy)
Grape - Esca (Black Measles).causes: Complex of several fungi (e.g., Phaeomoniella chlamydospora)
Grape - Esca (Black Measles).treatment: No cure for the vine. Pruning out infected wood can help manage it.
Grape - Esca (Black Measles).prevention: Protect pruning wounds with sealants. Avoid water stress.
Grape - Esca (Black Measles).immediate_action: Mark infected vines for selective pruning or removal.

Grape - Leaf Blight.name: Grape - Leaf Blight
symptoms: Leaf margins may turn brown and dry
Grape - Leaf Blight.treatment: Fungicide applications similar to Black Rot (Bordeaux mixture).
Grape - Leaf Blight.prevention: Remove leaf litter. Improve airflow in the vineyard.
Grape - Leaf Blight.immediate_action: Clean up fallen leaves to reduce spore load.

Orange - Haunglongbing (Citrus Greening).name: Citrus Greening (Haunglongbing)
symptoms: Yellowing of leaves, often in a blotchy pattern
Small, bitter, misshapen fruit that remains partially green
Orange - Haunglongbing (Citrus Greening).causes: Candidatus Liberibacter asiaticus (Bacteria, spread by Asian Citrus Psyllid)
Orange - Haunglongbing (Citrus Greening).treatment: No known cure. Manage the psyllid population. Removal of infected trees is necessary.
Orange - Haunglongbing (Citrus Greening).prevention: Use disease-free nursery stock. Monitor for psyllids using sticky traps. Use insecticidal soaps or oils for psyllid control.
Orange - Haunglongbing (Citrus Greening).immediate_action: Remove and destroy infected trees to prevent the spread of bacteria.

Peach - Bacterial Spot.name: Peach - Bacterial Spot
symptoms: Small, angular, water-soaked spots on leaf undersides
Spots turn purple or brown and drop out (shot-hole appearance)
Cracking and pitting on fruit surface
Peach - Bacterial Spot.treatment: Apply copper-based sprays during dormancy. Oxytetracycline during the season.
Peach - Bacterial Spot.prevention: Plant resistant cultivars. Avoid excessive nitrogen.
Peach - Bacterial Spot.immediate_action: Apply a recommended bactericide and avoid overhead irrigation.

Peach - Healthy.name: Peach - Healthy
Peach - Healthy.prevention: Regular pruning and preventative dormant sprays.
Peach - Healthy.immediate_action: Monitor for pests like peach tree borers.

Bell Pepper - Bacterial Spot.name: Bell Pepper - Bacterial Spot
symptoms: Spots turn brown with yellow halos
Bell Pepper - Bacterial Spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides. Use Streptomycin in some regions (check local regulations).
Bell Pepper - Bacterial Spot.prevention: Use pathogen-free seeds. Rotate crops every 3 years. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Bell Pepper - Bacterial Spot.immediate_action: Remove and destroy infected plants. Do not handle plants when wet.

Bell Pepper - Healthy.name: Bell Pepper - Healthy
symptoms: No signs of spots or yellowing
Bell Pepper - Healthy.prevention: Provide consistent moisture and adequate calcium to prevent blossom end rot.

Potato - Early Blight.name: Potato - Early Blight
symptoms: Small brown spots with concentric 'target' rings
Usually starts on older, lower leaves
Potato - Early Blight.treatment: Apply fungicides like Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb. Organic: Copper fungicides or Neem oil.
Potato - Early Blight.prevention: Rotate crops. Remove debris. Maintain plant vigor with proper nutrition.
Potato - Early Blight.immediate_action: Increase spacing between plants and apply an organic fungicide.

Potato - Late Blight.name: Potato - Late Blight
symptoms: Large, water-soaked dark green to black spots
White moldy growth on leaf undersides in humid conditions
Rapid wilting and rot of the entire plant
Potato - Late Blight.treatment: Apply systemic fungicides. Destroy infected plants immediately to prevent spread.
Potato - Late Blight.prevention: Use certified disease-free seed potatoes. Avoid overhead watering.
Potato - Late Blight.immediate_action: Remove and destroy all infected foliage and tubers immediately. DO NOT COMPOST.

Potato - Healthy.name: Potato - Healthy
symptoms: No visible leaf spots or yellowing

Raspberry - Healthy.name: Raspberry - Healthy
symptoms: Vibrant green leaves with serrated edges
Bright red or black berries (in season)
Raspberry - Healthy.prevention: Prune out old canes after harvest. Ensure good air circulation.

Soybean - Healthy.name: Soybean - Healthy
Soybean - Healthy.causes: Optimal nitrogen fixation and soil health.
Soybean - Healthy.prevention: Rotate with corn. Control weeds to reduce competition.

Squash - Powdery Mildew.name: Squash - Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White, flour-like patches on leaves and stems
Squash - Powdery Mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate. Organic: Neem oil or a mix of baking soda and water.
Squash - Powdery Mildew.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Ensure full sun and good air circulation.
Squash - Powdery Mildew.immediate_action: Prune heavily infected leaves and apply an organic fungicide.

Strawberry - Leaf Scorch.name: Strawberry - Leaf Scorch
symptoms: Small, dark-purple spots on upper leaf surface
Spots enlarge and merge, making the leaf look scorched or burned
Leaves may curl and turn brown
Strawberry - Leaf Scorch.treatment: Fungicide sprays (Captan or sulfur). Organic: Neem oil.
Strawberry - Leaf Scorch.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Remove old leaves after harvest. Improve drainage.
Strawberry - Leaf Scorch.immediate_action: Prune away heavily infected leaves and improve air circulation.

Strawberry - Healthy.name: Strawberry - Healthy
symptoms: White flowers and bright red fruit
Strawberry - Healthy.prevention: Mulching with straw to keep berries off the soil.

Tomato - Bacterial Spot.name: Tomato - Bacterial Spot
symptoms: Small, circular, greasy-looking spots on leaves
Small, raised black spots on fruit
Tomato - Bacterial Spot.treatment: Copper-based fungicides. Organic: Actigard or Serenade.
Tomato - Bacterial Spot.prevention: Avoid overhead watering. Rotate crops. Use clean seeds.
Tomato - Bacterial Spot.immediate_action: Remove infected leaves and apply copper spray.

Tomato - Early Blight.name: Tomato - Early Blight
symptoms: Sunken rot at the stem end of fruit
Tomato - Early Blight.treatment: Copper or sulfur fungicides. Prune lower leaves to improve airflow.
Tomato - Early Blight.prevention: Mulch around plants. Rotate crops. Avoid wetting foliage.
Tomato - Early Blight.immediate_action: Prune infected bottom leaves and apply a preventative fungicide.

Tomato - Late Blight.name: Tomato - Late Blight
symptoms: Large, olive-green to black water-soaked patches
White fungal growth on undersides in moist weather
Fruit develops large, firm, brown greasy spots
Tomato - Late Blight.treatment: Apply fungicides immediately (Chlorothalonil or Copper). Often fatal to the plant.
Tomato - Late Blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties (e.g., 'Defiant'). Keep foliage dry.
Tomato - Late Blight.immediate_action: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately to prevent neighborhood-wide spread.

Tomato - Leaf Mold.name: Tomato - Leaf Mold
symptoms: Pale green or yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces
Olive-green to brown velvety growth on leaf undersides
Tomato - Leaf Mold.treatment: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or copper. Improve greenhouse ventilation.
Tomato - Leaf Mold.prevention: Avoid high humidity. Plant resistant varieties. Prune for better airflow.
Tomato - Leaf Mold.immediate_action: Increase ventilation and apply a preventative fungicide.

Tomato - Septoria Leaf Spot.name: Tomato - Septoria Leaf Spot
symptoms: Numerous small, circular spots with gray centers and dark borders
Tiny black specks (fruiting bodies) in the centers
Usually starts on lower leaves and moves up
Tomato - Septoria Leaf Spot.prevention: Crop rotation and clearing debris. Mulching is critical.
Tomato - Septoria Leaf Spot.immediate_action: Remove infected leaves and apply an organic fungicide.

Tomato - Spider Mites (Two-Spotted Spide.name: Tomato - Spider Mites
symptoms: Fine stippling (tiny white or yellow dots) on leaves
Fine webbing on leaf undersides or between leaves
Tomato - Spider Mites (Two-Spotted Spide.treatment: Apply insecticidal soap or Neem oil. Release predatory mites (organic control).
Tomato - Spider Mites (Two-Spotted Spide.prevention: Keep plants well-watered (mites thrive in dry conditions). Monitor regularly with a magnifying glass.
Tomato - Spider Mites (Two-Spotted Spide.immediate_action: Spray plants with a strong stream of water to dislodge mites, then apply Neem oil.

Tomato - Target Spot.name: Tomato - Target Spot
symptoms: Small, brown, circular spots with concentric rings (like a target)
Spots may have a yellow halo
Pits or lesions on fruit surface
Tomato - Target Spot.treatment: Apply fungicides like azoxystrobin or boscalid. Copper fungicides can also help.
Tomato - Target Spot.prevention: Ensure good drainage and airflow. Remove plant debris after harvest.
Tomato - Target Spot.immediate_action: Prune affected leaves and apply a preventative fungicide.

Tomato - Yellow Leaf Curl Virus.name: Tomato - Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
Tomato - Yellow Leaf Curl Virus.treatment: No cure for the virus. Control the whitefly population.
Tomato - Yellow Leaf Curl Virus.prevention: Use reflective mulches. Plant resistant varieties. Use insect nets.
Tomato - Yellow Leaf Curl Virus.immediate_action: Remove and destroy infected plants to prevent the virus from spreading via whiteflies.

Tomato - Tomato Mosaic Virus.name: Tomato Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Mottling or mosaic patterns of light and dark green on leaves
Tomato - Tomato Mosaic Virus.treatment: No cure. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately.
Tomato - Tomato Mosaic Virus.prevention: Wash hands and sanitize tools after handling plants. Use virus-free seeds. Avoid smoking near plants (tobacco can carry the virus).
Tomato - Tomato Mosaic Virus.immediate_action: Remove infected plants and DO NOT touch healthy plants until tools/hands are sanitized.

Tomato - Healthy.name: Tomato - Healthy
symptoms: No signs of wilting or spots
Tomato - Healthy.prevention: Stake plants for support and airflow.
